diagrams.title: Binary Search Algorithm Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows the step-by-step process of binary search on a sorted array, demonstrating how the search space is halved with each comparison. The diagram displays the array elements, the low, high, and mid pointers, and highlights the portion of the array being searched.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the divide-and-conquer strategy of binary search and why it achieves O(log n) time complexity compared to linear search O(n)

diagrams.title: Quick Sort Partitioning Process
diagrams.description: Illustrates the partitioning step of Quick Sort algorithm showing the pivot selection, element swapping, and final pivot placement. The diagram shows an array being partitioned around a chosen pivot, with elements less than pivot moving left and greater elements moving right.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the divide-and-conquer approach of Quick Sort and how partitioning achieves average O(n log n) time complexity through recursive sorting of subarrays

diagrams.title: Merge Sort Recursion Tree
diagrams.description: Displays the recursive decomposition and merging process of Merge Sort as a tree structure. The diagram shows the top-down splitting phase where the array is divided into single elements, and the bottom-up merging phase where sorted subarrays are combined.
elements: arrows showing direction of splitting and merging
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes how Merge Sort repeatedly divides the problem and combines solutions, explaining the O(n log n) time complexity and the need for auxiliary space
